channel.title: Posts on Mare Blog — Visual Reference Management
channel.description: Recent content in Posts on Mare Blog — Visual Reference Management

item.title: Why Designers Are Leaving Pinterest in 2026: The Algorithm Problem
item.description: The real reasons designers are quitting Pinterest in 2026. Based on interviews with 500+ designers and 3 years of platform changes.

item.title: The Complete Guide to Pinterest Alternatives for Designers (2026)
item.description: The definitive guide to Pinterest alternatives for designers in 2026. Based on 500+ designer interviews and 30 days of hands-on testing with every major tool.

item.title: How to Break Your Pinterest Addiction: A Designer's Guide to Reclaiming Creativity
item.description: 73% of designers spend 2-4 hours daily on Pinterest. Learn the 5-step plan to reclaim your creativity and stop the endless scroll.

item.title: Are.na Alternative: Why Visual Designers Are Switching to Mare
item.description:
Are.na Alternative: Why Visual Designers Are Switching to Mare

The Problem with Are.na

Are.na is beautiful. It’s minimal, thoughtful, and appeals to the “slow web” aesthetic. But for working designers, it has critical limitations:

  • No quick capture - Adding references requires too many clicks
  • No visual search - Can’t find “that blue gradient from 3 months ago”
  • Limited organization - Channels are flat, no nested collections
  • No client sharing - Can’t create private share links for mood boards

What Designers Actually Need

After talking to 50+ visual designers who tried Are.na and left, here’s what they wanted:

item.title: Cosmos.so Alternative: When Visual World-Building Needs Structure
item.description:
Cosmos.so Alternative: When Visual World-Building Needs Structure

The Cosmos Appeal

Cosmos.so promises “visual world-building for creatives.” It’s dreamy, spatial, and feels like exploring a memory palace. But professional designers hit walls:

  • No search - You must browse your collections spatially
  • No hierarchy - Everything exists on the same infinite plane
  • Performance issues - Large collections lag significantly
  • No export - Your work is trapped in their ecosystem

The Reality of Professional Reference Management

Cosmos works for:

item.title: Pinterest Alternative for Professional Designers
item.description:
Pinterest Alternative for Professional Designers

Why Designers Outgrow Pinterest

Pinterest is the world’s largest inspiration database. But it’s built for consumers, not creators:

The Algorithm Problem

Pinterest shows you what it thinks you want. As a designer, you need:

  • Exact search - “Bauhaus posters with primary colors”
  • Chronological discovery - “What did I save last Tuesday?”
  • Source preservation - “Where did this image come from?”

Instead, you get:

  • Algorithmic recommendations (not what you searched)
  • Endless scroll (no sense of time)
  • Broken source links (original context lost)

The Organization Problem

Pinterest boards are flat. Design projects are complex:
